patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH v4 0/4] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

version_history.subject: [PATCH v3 0/4] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE
[PATCH v4 0/4] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 1/4] mm: track DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback
series_items.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new per-writeback-domain (bdi) counter to track the number of dirty pages that have been dirtied via RWF_DONTCACHE writes, also known as 'DONTCACHE' dirty pages. The counter is used by the writeback flusher to determine how many pages to write back when expediting writeback for IOCB_DONTCACHE writes. This approach aims to optimize writeback performance for specific use cases.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary: The patch looks good and the author is welcome to add a Reviewed-by tag from Jan Kara.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 2/4] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's writeback behavior to improve performance in the IOCB_DONTCACHE path. The original code submitted writeback requests inline, causing significant latency and contention. This patch introduces a background flusher that writes back dirty pages in the background, reducing the impact on the writer's hot path. To limit unnecessary flushing, it uses a dedicated bit and counter to target only the necessary dirty pages. The result is improved throughput and reduced tail latency for sequential write workloads.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch description should include a Reviewed-by tag from themselves, and added their own Reviewed-by line to the comment

Jan Kara pointed out that the patch description inaccurately states that only dontcache pages are written back, when in fact arbitrary dirty pages are written back based on the WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY counter.
review_comments.raw_body:
Nice and looks good to me now. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

One nit below:

---

This comment is a bit confusing as in fact we write arbitrary dirty pages.
It is only the amount of pages that is influenced by WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY. So
I'd rephrase the last sentence like: We queue writeback for the inode's wb
for as many pages as there are dontcache pages but we don't restrict
writeback to dontcache pages only. This significantly improves performance
over either writing all wb's pages or writing only dontcache pages.
Although it doesn't guarantee quick writeback and reclaim of dontcache
pages it keeps the amount of dirty pages in check and over longer term
dontcache pages get written and reclaimed by background writeback even with
this rough heuristic.

								Honza

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 3/4] testing: add nfsd-io-bench NFS server benchmark suite
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a benchmark suite to test NFSD I/O mode performance using fio against an NFS server on localhost. The suite includes various workloads such as sequential and random read/write, multi-writer, noisy-neighbor, and latency-sensitive reader tests. It also includes scripts for setting up the NFS export, running the benchmarks, and parsing the results. This patch aims to provide a comprehensive testing framework for evaluating NFSD I/O mode performance.

series_items.subject: [PATCH v4 4/4] testing: add dontcache-bench local filesystem benchmark suite
series_items.patch_summary: This patch adds a new benchmark suite called 'dontcache-bench' to test the IOCB_DONTCACHE flag on local filesystems using fio's io_uring engine. The suite includes various tests, such as competing buffered vs. dontcache writers and a benchmark mirroring Jens Axboe's original RWF_UNCACHED write test. The patch also includes job files for fio, scripts to run the benchmarks and parse results, and makes changes to several files in the kernel source tree.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series aims to improve write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by implementing a scheme that kicks the flusher thread to do an amount of writeback proportional to the amount written after a DONTCACHE write. The approach was suggested by Jan Kara and Christoph Hellwig during review of earlier versions. The patches track dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kick the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and add mechanisms for running 'perf lock' around test cases. Benchmark results show marked improvement in RWF_DONTCACHE write performance across various tests.

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a DONTCACHE write is followed by an fsync, which can cause data corruption.

Jan Kara pointed out that the patch description inaccurately states that only dirty dontcache pages are written back, when in fact arbitrary dirty pages are written back, but the amount is influenced by WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY. He suggested rephrasing the sentence to clarify this and noted that this heuristic improves performance over other approaches.
review_comments.raw_body:
Nice and looks good to me now. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

One nit below:

---

This comment is a bit confusing as in fact we write arbitrary dirty pages.
It is only the amount of pages that is influenced by WB_DONTCACHE_DIRTY. So
I'd rephrase the last sentence like: We queue writeback for the inode's wb
for as many pages as there are dontcache pages but we don't restrict
writeback to dontcache pages only. This significantly improves performance
over either writing all wb's pages or writing only dontcache pages.
Although it doesn't guarantee quick writeback and reclaim of dontcache
pages it keeps the amount of dirty pages in check and over longer term
dontcache pages get written and reclaimed by background writeback even with
this rough heuristic.

								Honza

review_comments.author: Jan Kara
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch series does not include a mechanism to handle concurrent DONTCACHE writes, which could lead to writeback issues and requested the addition of such a mechanism.
review_comments.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza
